title: video-to-gif project configuration
description: Schema for .video-to-gif.json (spec section 9). Unknown fields are warned about rather than rejected, so additionalProperties is intentionally left permissive; the engine emits a warning for any field not defined here. Configuration MUST NOT contain credentials, tokens, signed URLs, passwords, private keys, shell commands, or executable hooks (spec section 9.4).

schemaVersion.description: Configuration schema version. Must be 1 for this release.

defaultProfile.description: Default quality profile used when no request-specific profile is given.

outputDirectory.description: Directory GIFs are written to; created if missing. Defaults to ./output. Writing outside the project root requires allowOutsideProject plus explicit approval.

loop.description: Loop behavior. 'forever' loops infinitely; 'once' equals a count of 1; an integer N >= 1 plays N times. 0 is rejected.

collisionPolicy.description: How to handle an existing output file. 'ask' is the skill-layer policy (engine reports collisions, the agent asks, then re-runs with an explicit engine policy). Engine policies are fail, overwrite, unique, skip. Default fail at the engine; ask at the skill layer.

continueOnError.description: When true, a runtime failure on one clip does not stop remaining clips in a batch.

keepTemporaryFiles.description: Debug only. When true, palette and temporary files are kept instead of being cleaned up. Disabled by default.

allowOutsideProject.description: Must be true (and explicitly approved) before the engine writes outside the project root.

remoteSources.description: Remote source acquisition policy (spec FR-018, section 9.5). 'disabled' (default) rejects remote URLs with REMOTE_DISABLED (exit 8) and performs no network access; 'ask' requires explicit user approval before each remote acquisition; 'enabled' permits remote acquisition without a per-request prompt. Enablement alone does not authorize private-network addresses or bypass the URL scheme allowlist.

keepRemoteSource.description: When true, a downloaded remote source is retained after the job and its path is reported in the structured result. Disabled by default. Equivalent to the --keep-remote-source flag (spec FR-020, section 9.5).

transformations.description: Global transformation defaults (spec section 9.6, FR-026..FR-028). Every field is optional and additive; they do not change schemaVersion. A configuration that omits this object behaves as though the documented defaults were supplied. NOTE: 'crop' is deliberately absent and is NOT permitted here — a crop rectangle is only meaningful against one specific source's dimensions, so validate-config rejects a 'crop' key with a validation error at field path transformations.crop (exit 2). Supply a crop per request (--crop) or per clip in a manifest. validate-config applies every source-independent check below; source-dependent checks (crop bounds, upscale evaluation) happen during preflight.

width.description: Maximum output width in pixels, 2 to 8192 (FR-026). null means the effective quality profile's maximum width applies. An out-of-range value fails with INVALID_DIMENSIONS (exit 6).

height.description: Maximum output height in pixels, 2 to 8192 (FR-026). null means the height follows the effective source aspect ratio. An out-of-range value fails with INVALID_DIMENSIONS (exit 6).

speed.description: Playback speed multiplier, 0.25 to 4.0 with at most three fractional digits (FR-027). 1.0 leaves timing unchanged. An invalid value fails with INVALID_SPEED (exit 6).

dither.description: Palette-use dither mode (FR-028). null means the effective quality profile's default mode (section 15.5): bayer with bayerScale 5 for 'small', sierra2_4a otherwise. An unknown value fails with INVALID_DITHER (exit 6).

bayerScale.description: Bayer matrix scale, 0 to 5 (FR-028). Meaningful only when the effective dither mode is 'bayer'; supplying it explicitly under any other mode fails with INVALID_DITHER (exit 6). null means the profile default applies (5 for 'small'), falling back to 2.

limits.description: Resource-safety and download limits (spec SEC-011, FR-021, section 9.5).

maxClipProcessingSeconds.description: Per-clip wall-clock processing timeout in seconds. Default 600. Exceeding it fails with RESOURCE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED (exit 13).

maxTemporaryBytes.description: Ceiling on temporary-disk usage per job, in bytes. Default 2147483648 (2 GiB). Exceeding it fails with RESOURCE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED (exit 13). Remote downloads count toward this accounting.

maxDownloadBytes.description: Maximum size of a single remote download, in bytes, enforced on bytes actually received during streaming (not on a declared Content-Length). Default 2147483648 (2 GiB). Exceeding it fails with REMOTE_TOO_LARGE (exit 13) and leaves no partial file. Remote acquisition only (v0.2.0).

maxDownloadSeconds.description: Download wall-clock timeout in seconds. Default 900. Exceeding it aborts the download with REMOTE_DOWNLOAD_FAILED (exit 14) and leaves no partial file. Remote acquisition only (v0.2.0).
